items.title: Visa, Mastercard and Ripple back x402 as agent payments average 32 cents
items.description: Forty companies now govern x402, the protocol Coinbase built and handed away. It settled about $24 million last month across 75 million payments.

items.title: Bitcoin nears $65,000 as cooling U.S. inflation guts the Fed rate-hike trade
items.description: The June CPI print pulled hike odds from 43% to 13%, with analysts now watching the September FOMC meeting for further cues on positioning.

items.title: U.S. CFTC moves to stop Kalshi from canceling trades as ordered by Michigan court
items.description: The federal derivatives regulator, which oversees trading at Kalshi, argued it was inappropriate for Michigan to "bully" the firm into reversing trades.

items.title: Some U.S. Senate Democrats come out against Clarity Act, calling it a 'corrupt' bill
items.description: Certain Democrats are taking an increasingly rigorous stand against the crypto market structure bill, which needs a significant number from their party to pass.

items.title: Binance bets on becoming a crypto 'super app' as stablecoins reshape growth
items.description: Shunyet Jan, Binance’s head of spot trading and derivatives, said the exchange is focusing more on payments and financial services than on trading alone as it drives the next phase of its expansion.

items.title: The Clarity Act isn't a ticket to sanctions evasion, actually
items.description: The bill, as currently drafted, has the power to stop sanctions evasion at scale, argues Ari Redbord, global head of policy at TRM Labs.

items.title: Mizuho downgrades Circle to underperform, cuts price target to $50 on Open USD threat
items.description: The Japanese investment bank said Open USD's yield pass-through model could pressure Circle's margins by shifting more reserve income to distributors.

items.title: Wikipedia blackout could hurt how AI engines like ChatGPT understand crypto
items.description: Wikipedia observes guidelines on crypto coverage that date back many years and may now be outdated, according to the report.

items.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
items.description: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.

items.title: Kalshi says CFTC, Michigan orders leave it in ‘impossible position’
items.description: “We are disappointed by this decision and believe it is unfair to Kalshi,” the company’s legal counsel said on X.

items.title: US freezes $131M in Iran-linked crypto as Middle East tensions rise
items.description: “US Treasury is committed to disrupting and degrading Iran’s illicit financial activities, including its abuse of digital assets,” US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said Tuesday.

items.title: Bitmine generated $46M from Ethereum staking last quarter
items.description: Ethereum staking generated 98% of Bitmine’s revenue last quarter as the company’s pivot from Bitcoin mining gained momentum following its March validator launch.

items.title: Over 95% of Coinbase’s code is now written with help of AI
items.description: Coinbase’s Rob Witoff said the company is leaning more on AI to help with execution, but still needs high-agency humans for judgment and strategy.

items.title: Three US senators oppose CLARITY Act on ethics grounds with vote expected soon
items.description: Senate Majority Leader John Thune reportedly said the chamber will consider a vote on the crypto market structure bill before Aug. 10, though it’s unclear whether it has enough Democratic support to pass.

items.title: Velocity raises $38M to build stablecoin treasury infrastructure for enterprises
items.description: Backed by Dragonfly, FirstMark and Coinbase Ventures, the startup is expanding software that helps businesses integrate stablecoins into treasury and payment workflows.

items.title: US, UK treasuries to align transatlantic rules on tokenization and stablecoins
items.description: The two governments issued recommendations on the treatment of digital assets as the US prepares to implement a 2025 law on payment stablecoins.

items.title: ASML jumps over 7% after hiking sales forecast for second time this year on strong AI chip demand
items.description: ASML on Wednesday raised its guidance for a second time this year as its customers continue to ramp up their production capacity of AI chips.

items.title: Oil rises after another round of U.S. strikes against Iran, naval blockade of the country's ports
items.description: Oil prices rose Wednesday after U.S. forces struck Tehran and Washington reinstated its naval blockade of Iranian ports near the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: China posts slowest quarterly growth since 2022 as investment slumps, fanning stimulus calls
items.description: That second-quarter growth came below Beijing's full-year growth target range of 4.5% to 5%, the least ambitious goal in decades.

items.title: Why Europe is suddenly betting big on drones
items.description: Drones and autonomous systems are moving from niche battlefield tools to a core part of modern warfare.

items.title: The AI boom just found two new winners: Goldman Sachs and JPMorgan Chase
items.description: Goldman Sachs and JPMorgan showed that Wall Street is a major beneficiary of the AI boom, with record revenue driven by surging trading and investment banking.

items.title: U.S. strikes Iranian missile sites, defense systems and reinstates port blockade
items.description: President Donald Trump has said the U.S.-Iran ceasefire is over following multiple flare-ups of hostilities in the region.

items.title: SK Hynix South Korean shares jump 11% as Asia tech stocks rally
items.description: SK Hynix led a broad rally in Asian technology shares on Wednesday, tracking a rebound in U.S. semiconductor shares after a sharp selloff earlier this week.

items.title: IBM stock craters 25%, the worst day on record, after company issues second-quarter earnings warning
items.description: The CEO blamed the shortfall on weakness in the software and infrastructure business because clients shifted money toward hardware purchases.

items.transcript_reason:
Could not retrieve a transcript for the video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=prxbky7kj5o! This is most likely caused by:

YouTube is blocking requests from your IP. This usually is due to one of the following reasons:
- You have done too many requests and your IP has been blocked by YouTube
- You are doing requests from an IP belonging to a cloud provider (like AWS, Google Cloud Platform, Azure, etc.). Unfortunately, most IPs from cloud providers are blocked by YouTube.

There are two things you can do to work around this:
1. Use proxies to hide your IP address, as explained in the "Working around IP bans" section of the README (https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api?tab=readme-ov-file#working-around-ip-bans-requestblocked-or-ipblocked-exception).
2. (NOT RECOMMENDED) If you authenticate your requests using cookies, you will be able to continue doing requests for a while. However, YouTube will eventually permanently ban the account that you have used to authenticate with! So only do this if you don't mind your account being banned!

If you are sure that the described cause is not responsible for this error and that a transcript should be retrievable, please create an issue at https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api/issues. Please add which version of youtube_transcript_api you are using and provide the information needed to replicate the error. Also make sure that there are no open issues which already describe your problem!

items.title: I’m 67 with a $140,000 pension. Should I wait until 70 to claim Social Security so my wife gets more?
items.description: “When I pass, all my retirement income is reduced to $30,000 a year.”

items.title: Brace for $4 gas again: How U.S.-Iran tensions are threatening to end the price break at the pump
items.description: Americans got some relief at the gas pump in recent weeks, enough to slow down inflation. That might not last long as the U.S. and Iran continue to vie for control of the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: This overlooked index is a better investment than the S&P 500
items.description: We did the math over a 30-year period, and it’s not even close.

items.title: ‘It’s heartbreaking’: My brother claimed Social Security at 70. He died from cancer after one payment. Why wait to claim?
items.description: “I’ve always been a little skeptical of the government’s encouragement to delay claiming benefits.”

items.title: Your monthly Netflix bill is up 29% in just over a year. It’s time for Washington to step in.
items.description: Netflix is still a Wall Street favorite — and a target for government regulators

items.title: Three powerful forces are draining family wealth — and your estate plan is completely unprepared
items.description: From Medicaid cuts to an IRA tax trap, here are the real threats to your family’s inheritance and what to do about them.

items.title: What IBM’s profit warning means: Hardware is ‘eating everyone’s lunch’
items.description: The company said the shortfall in its software and infrastructure business was tied to clients spending on memory ahead of price hikes.

items.title: Taco Bell reportedly being investigated by health officials, as cyclosporiasis outbreak spreads
items.description: Shares of Taco Bell parent Yum Brands were down nearly 4% on Tuesday.

items.title: Silicon shadows: inside the black market for AI chips | FT Film

- You are doing requests from an IP belonging to a cloud provider (like AWS, Google Cloud Platform, Azure, etc.). Unfortunately, most IPs from cloud providers are blocked by YouTube.

There are two things you can do to work around this:
1. Use proxies to hide your IP address, as explained in the "Working around IP bans" section of the README (https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api?tab=readme-ov-file#working-around-ip-bans-requestblocked-or-ipblocked-exception).
2. (NOT RECOMMENDED) If you authenticate your requests using cookies, you will be able to continue doing requests for a while. However, YouTube will eventually permanently ban the account that you have used to authenticate with! So only do this if you don't mind your account being banned!

If you are sure that the described cause is not responsible for this error and that a transcript should be retrievable, please create an issue at https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api/issues. Please add which version of youtube_transcript_api you are using and provide the information needed to replicate the error. Also make sure that there are no open issues which already describe your problem!

items.title: Stripe and Advent make $53bn bid for PayPal
items.description: Offer by payments start-up and private equity group comes at 28% premium to Tuesday’s closing share price

items.title: China’s economy grows at one of lowest rates in decades
items.description: Second-quarter GDP figure falls below annual target range as economic pressure mounts

items.title: Wall Street banks are AI stocks now
items.description: Plus a nice cool CPI report

items.title: Brussels has delivered only a slice of Draghi reforms, report says
items.description: Also in this newsletter: UAE presses Europe to play a bigger role in Gulf security

items.title: What could go wrong
items.description: Assessing the world economy’s resilience

items.title: Chipmaker CXMT seeks $10bn in largest China IPO since 2010
items.description: Chinese group capitalises on boom in demand for AI memory chips

items.title: FirstFT: Ukraine to buy Chinese drone parts with EU funds
items.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Trump’s return to war with Iran and BYD signals wider European expansion

items.title: The Story of Money
items.description: Watch the latest episode from the FT podcast exploring the history of finance

items.title: China’s Economy Grows at Slowest Pace in Years
items.description: Economic growth of 4.3 percent in the second quarter, versus the same period last year, reflected a broad slump outside of the country’s export-oriented manufacturing might.

items.title: Tech Investors and Longevity Influencers Back Neko Health’s Mega Round
items.description: The health tech start-up co-founded by Spotify’s Daniel Ek is worth billions more after a new $700 million funding round.

items.title: Data Centers to Add Billions in Power Costs in 13 States
items.description: A power auction conducted by a giant grid operator is expected to add $6.3 billion in additional charges to consumers and businesses because of electricity needs of data centers.

items.title: Public Health Groups Sue F.D.A. Over Flavored E-Cigarette Policy
items.description: Executives unhappy with the agency donated and lobbied President Trump directly. Major companies have already begun to prepare and ship new products.

items.title: Top Tax Official to Leave Trump Administration
items.description: Ken Kies, who held two top jobs at the Treasury Department and the I.R.S., is leaving the government.

items.title: Trump Flip-Flops on Strait of Hormuz, Creating New Risks for Global Economy
items.description: The president now wants Gulf nations to invest in the U.S. in exchange for safe passage. Shipping industry experts worry other nations will follow that lead.

items.title: Chipotle Is Bringing Its Mexican-Inspired Fast Food to Monterrey, Mexico
items.description: The California-based restaurant chain is opening its first location south of the U.S.-Mexico border this week, bringing its bowls and burritos to Monterrey.

items.title: Leonard Abramson, Health Care Innovator and Philanthropist, Dies at 93
items.description: He created U.S. Healthcare, one of the first H.M.O.s, and used his great wealth from the sale of the company for causes like advancing cancer research.
